selection.status_reason: Live thesis monitor refreshed the active weekly pick.

reasons: Price trend remains constructive: 5-day momentum is 1.8% and 20-day momentum is 35.1%.
Trend quality is supported by 80% positive sessions over the last 10 trading days and the price sitting 18.2% relative to its 20-day average.
Risk profile shows daily volatility around 3.4%, downside volatility near 2.0%, and a recent max drawdown of 3.8%.
Volume confirmation is -18.2% versus the trailing baseline, which helps separate steady breakouts from thin momentum.
Relative strength is 0.6% versus SPY over 5 days and -1.1% versus the sector ETF.
News signal is bullish with score 0.68, confidence 0.41, and 0.5 weighted articles across 4 sources.
GPT-5 article classification and company-news provider coverage rated the news flow bullish at 0.82, after adjusting for relevance, recency, source quality, and story concentration.
Evidence quality: 4 articles across 4 sources, 0.5 effective weighted articles, average relevance 0.87, average source quality 0.81, confidence 0.74.
Broad market news leaned bearish for the Technology sector with score 0.26 and confidence 0.54.

University of Michigan consumer sentiment at a record low implies a weaker near-term consumer demand backdrop; modestly higher crude raises fuel costs and reduces disposable income. Transmission to markets: weaker consumer demand and rising cost pressures can depress sales of consumer-facing tech products and advertising/consumer services, and raise the risk that corporates pause discretionary IT spend if macro concerns deepen. Effects are broad and modest over the next 1-15 trading days; not company-specific to tracked symbols, so assign a mild near-term negative bias to the technology sector with low-to-moderate confidence.
Sector overlay was trimmed by 35% because it overlaps with company or macro themes.
Technical weights were calibrated on 2303 historical symbol-weeks against 5-day excess return (IC 0.00).
Overall model score is 0.103 with medium confidence.

news_evidence.title: Cisco Systems Inc. filed 10-Q: 10-Q
news_evidence.llm_reason: Official SEC EDGAR 10-Q filing for Cisco. Company filings are materially relevant for short-term trading because they can contain updated results, risk disclosures, guidance changes, or other items that market participants react to.

news_evidence.title: Palo Alto Networks Up 70%: Can the Rally Last Into June?
news_evidence.llm_reason: Coverage of Palo Alto Networks’ rally. Relevant as sector/peer momentum (cybersecurity/software) that could bleed into Cisco’s security-related businesses, but not a direct company-specific catalyst for CSCO over the next 1–10 trading days.
